title: Excel Power Pivot DAX compatibility and ratio stability
originSignal: Measures that are valid in Power BI can fail in Excel Power Pivot or become unstable when ratios divide by zero.
riskPattern: Using Power BI-only functions, clearing filters from the wrong table, or using direct division in ratios can produce errors or misleading values in Excel.
regressionChecks: Prefer Excel Power Pivot-compatible filter clearing patterns.
Flag version-sensitive functions before writing formulas into an Excel Data Model.
Trace downstream measure dependencies before rename or delete operations.
expectedEvidence: DAX compatibility lint report blocks unsupported formulas and records warnings for version-sensitive formulas.
DAX dependency report identifies upstream and downstream measure relationships.
Model report records tables, measures, relationships, and compatibility boundaries.
boundaries: Static linting does not prove the business measure is semantically correct.
Final DAX behavior must be validated in the target Excel Power Pivot workbook or compatible host.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Add a sanitized model export containing compatible ratios, an intentionally unsupported function, and a downstream dependency chain.
